headline: Italian Drama 'The Last One for the Road' Dominates National Film Awards
lede: Francesco Sossai's sophomore feature swept Italy's prestigious David di Donatello Awards, capturing eight major statuettes and cementing its place in the national cinematic landscape.
body:
In a stunning display of artistic prowess, **"The Last One for the Road"** emerged as the unequivocal champion at Italy's most celebrated film honors. The quirky dramatic feature, which originally premiered at Cannes' Un Certain Regard section, demonstrated remarkable crossover appeal by capturing awards across multiple categories.

Sossai's nuanced exploration of two aging small-time criminals and their unexpected friendship with a timid architecture student resonated deeply with the Italian film industry's most discerning voters. The film's narrative complexity and understated character development seemingly struck a powerful chord with the David di Donatello jury.

The awards sweep represents a significant breakthrough for Sossai, transforming the director from a promising emerging talent to a formidable force in contemporary Italian cinema. Each of the eight awards underscores the film's multifaceted artistic achievements, ranging from technical craft to narrative innovation.

Industry insiders are already speculating about the film's potential international trajectory, with several European distributors reportedly expressing keen interest in broader theatrical release strategies.
key_facts: Eight David di Donatello Awards won
Premiered at Cannes' Un Certain Regard
